rows.finalText: "What is the next right step in my calling" is best read here through Purpose / Calling with a right path emphasis. The sequence begins in Past with Scythe (10), so urgent prioritization is required is already in play. Here, Scythe emphasizes the necessary cut, refusal, or clean boundary that separates vocation from habit, urgency, or inherited expectation. Present centers on Mice (23), where small drains can undermine major goals becomes the point that can redirect the whole sequence. Mice often points to the small repeated drain that weakens conviction or meaning if it is left unaddressed in your calling. Sun (31) in Future suggests energy and confidence support forward movement, once the middle-card pressure has been worked with directly. Sun colors your calling through confidence, visibility, success, and the warming effect of momentum that is finally obvious. What ties the vocational pattern together is the link between Scythe (10) and Mice (23): a blend of cut and erosion, asking you to choose cleanly and communicate implications while staying alert to ignoring minor losses until they accumulate. The spread turns on Mice (23) more than any other card. Here, Mice emphasizes the small repeated drain that weakens conviction or meaning if it is left unaddressed. Scythe (10) with Mice (23) keep emphasizing a blend of cut and erosion, asking you to choose cleanly and communicate implications while staying alert to ignoring minor losses until they accumulate. Patch leaks and simplify so Sun (31) can land as lived experience rather than just possibility.
